description: Production-ready upgrade: Turso DB, NextAuth, map-based site picker, winds aloft scoring, Web Push notifications, PWA support. Zero-cost deployment on Vercel.

userStories.title: Turso + Drizzle Database Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to replace JSON file storage with Turso and Drizzle ORM so the app works on serverless platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @libsql/client and drizzle-orm + drizzle-kit
Create Drizzle schema in src/db/schema.ts with tables: users, accounts, sessions, sites, forecasts_cache, settings, push_subscriptions
Sites table: id, userId, name, latitude, longitude, elevation, idealWindDirections (JSON text), maxWindSpeed, notes, createdAt, updatedAt
Forecasts cache table: id, siteId, fetchDate, data (JSON text), fetchedAt, expiresAt
Settings table: id, userId, minScoreThreshold default 70, daysAhead default 2, siteNotifications (JSON text), updatedAt
Push subscriptions table: id, userId, endpoint, keys (JSON text), createdAt
src/db/index.ts initializes Turso client using TURSO_DATABASE_URL and TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN env vars
drizzle.config.ts configured for migrations
Generate initial migration with drizzle-kit generate
Remove old src/lib/storage.ts and all data/ directory references
Add .env.example with all required env vars
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use drizzle-orm/libsql. Schema must include NextAuth tables (users, accounts, sessions) for the adapter. Use text columns with JSON for arrays/objects since SQLite has no native JSON type. Use crypto.randomUUID() for IDs.

userStories.title: NextAuth Authentication
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign in with Google or GitHub so my sites and settings are saved to my account.
acceptanceCriteria: Install next-auth (Auth.js v5) and @auth/drizzle-adapter
Configure NextAuth in src/auth.ts with Drizzle adapter for Turso
Google and GitHub OAuth providers via env vars (AUTH_GOOGLE_ID, AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET, AUTH_GITHUB_ID, AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET)
Auth middleware in middleware.ts protecting /sites, /settings, and mutation API routes
Dashboard (/) remains publicly accessible
Sign in and sign out buttons in app header showing user avatar and name when signed in
Session provider wrapping the app layout
Auth env vars documented in .env.example
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use next-auth v5 beta (Auth.js). The Drizzle adapter needs the schema tables from US-001. Use middleware matcher to protect routes. Sign-in page can use the default next-auth pages or a custom one.

userStories.title: Migrate Site Management to Turso
userStories.description: As a user, I want my sites stored in the database so they persist across devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/app/sites/actions.ts to use Drizzle queries instead of JSON file operations
All CRUD operations work with Turso (create, read, update, delete sites)
Sites scoped to authenticated user via userId foreign key
Site form fields unchanged from v1 (name, lat, lng, elevation, wind dirs, max wind, notes)
Form validation unchanged
Sites page redirects to sign-in if not authenticated
Remove initializeSeedData from site actions (seeds handled separately in US-012)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use auth() from src/auth.ts to get the current user session. idealWindDirections stored as JSON text in SQLite, parse/stringify when reading/writing.

userStories.title: Migrate Settings to Turso
userStories.description: As a user, I want my notification settings stored in the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/app/settings/actions.ts to use Drizzle queries
Settings scoped to authenticated user
Per-site notification toggles stored as JSON text column (Record<siteId, boolean>)
Default settings row created on first access if none exists for user
Settings page redirects to sign-in if not authenticated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Settings is a simple key-value per user. Use upsert pattern (insert on conflict update).

userStories.title: Fix Weather Fetching + Add Winds Aloft
userStories.description: As a pilot, I want weather data that includes upper-air conditions so I can better assess XC potential.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Open-Meteo hourly parameters: boundary_layer_height, wind_speed_850hPa, wind_direction_850hPa, convective_inhibition
Update Forecast TypeScript types in src/types/index.ts to include new fields
Cache forecasts in Turso forecasts_cache table instead of JSON file
Cache key: siteId + fetchDate (same pattern as v1)
Cache expiration: 6 hours (same as v1)
Handle missing upper-air fields gracefully (default to null, scoring handles nulls)
Remove old forecasts.json references
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Open-Meteo API params to add: boundary_layer_height,wind_speed_850hPa,wind_direction_850hPa,convective_inhibition. These are all available in the standard forecast endpoint. 850hPa is roughly 1500m altitude - relevant for XC flying.

userStories.title: Fix Scoring Algorithm
userStories.description: As a pilot, I want accurate scoring that properly handles wind direction and includes upper-air data.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix wind direction averaging to use circular mean: atan2(mean of sin(dirs), mean of cos(dirs))
Add boundary layer height scoring: 0m=0, 500m=30, 1000m=60, 1500m=80, 2000m+=100
Revised weights: CAPE 25%, surface wind 20%, wind direction 15%, cloud cover 10%, precipitation 5%, BLH 15%, upper wind 10%
Flyable window uses timezone from Open-Meteo response (already in auto mode)
Handle null values for new parameters (use neutral score 50 if missing)
Score labels unchanged: Poor 0-30, Fair 31-50, Good 51-70, Great 71-85, Epic 86-100
DayScore type updated to include blh and upperWind factors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Circular mean formula: avgDir = atan2(mean(sin(radians(dirs))), mean(cos(radians(dirs)))) converted back to degrees. BLH (boundary layer height) directly correlates with thermal depth - higher is better for XC. 850hPa wind (roughly 1500m) affects safety and drift.

userStories.title: Dashboard Updates
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to show my sites or demo sites if not logged in.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users see their own sites with full interactivity
Unauthenticated users see 3 demo seed sites (Tiger Mountain, Dog Mountain, Chelan Butte) read-only with real weather data
Score detail dialog shows all 7 scoring factors including BLH and upper wind
Notification bell indicator reads from Turso settings
Refresh button works with Turso-cached forecasts
Loading skeletons while data fetches
Empty state for authenticated users with no sites
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Demo sites use hardcoded coordinates (no DB needed). Fetch weather live for demo sites. Show a banner for unauthenticated users: 'Sign in to add your own flying sites'.

userStories.title: Map-Based Site Picker
userStories.description: As a pilot, I want to pick my flying site on a map so I don't need to know exact coordinates.
acceptanceCriteria: Install leaflet and react-leaflet packages
Add interactive map component to the Add/Edit Site dialog
Clicking map sets latitude and longitude (auto-fills coordinate inputs)
Search box on map using Nominatim geocoding API (free, no key needed) to find locations by name
Existing coordinates shown as marker when editing a site
Map shown on site detail page with site marker
Map works on mobile with touch interactions
Leaflet CSS imported properly (common gotcha with Next.js)
Dynamic import for map component (Leaflet requires window/document)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Leaflet MUST be dynamically imported in Next.js (no SSR). Use next/dynamic with ssr: false. OpenStreetMap tiles are free. Nominatim has a 1 req/sec rate limit - add debounce to search. Use react-leaflet MapContainer, TileLayer, Marker, useMapEvents.

userStories.title: Site Detail Page Improvements
userStories.description: As a pilot, I want richer detail views with the new weather parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: Hourly data table includes boundary layer height and 850hPa wind columns
Score breakdown shows all 7 factors with labeled progress bars
Daily cards show BLH alongside wind, CAPE, cloud cover, precip
Hourly chart shows BLH as a third data layer (in addition to wind and CAPE)
Wind direction indicator shows ideal vs actual direction comparison
Site detail page fetches data from Turso via server actions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BLH displayed in meters (typical range 0-3000m). 850hPa wind in km/h. Keep the chart simple - CSS bars or SVG, no heavy chart libraries.

userStories.title: Web Push Notifications
userStories.description: As a pilot, I want to receive push notifications when good flying conditions are forecasted.
acceptanceCriteria: Install web-push package for server-side push
Generate VAPID keys and document in .env.example (NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY)
Create public/sw.js service worker that handles push events and notification clicks
Enable Notifications button on Settings page triggers browser permission and stores subscription in Turso
API route POST /api/notifications/check: fetches forecasts for all users sites, scores them, sends push to users meeting threshold
Notification shows: site name, date, score and label
Clicking notification opens /sites/[id] detail page
Vercel cron config in vercel.json: calls /api/notifications/check at 06:00 and 18:00 UTC daily
Disable/unsubscribe button on Settings page removes push subscription
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: web-push npm package handles VAPID signing. Service worker must be in public/ directory. Use navigator.serviceWorker.register and PushManager.subscribe on client. Cron endpoint should be protected with CRON_SECRET env var.

userStories.title: PWA Support
userStories.description: As a mobile pilot, I want to install SoarCast as an app on my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: Create public/manifest.json with name SoarCast, short_name SoarCast, theme_color, background_color, display standalone, start_url /
Generate PWA icons at 192x192 and 512x512 sizes (can be simple SVG-based or emoji-based like the existing icon.tsx)
Service worker (from US-010) also caches app shell assets for offline loading
Meta tags in layout.tsx: theme-color, apple-mobile-web-app-capable, apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style
Link to manifest.json in layout.tsx head
App installable on Android Chrome and iOS Safari
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Minimal PWA requires: manifest.json with icons + service worker + HTTPS (Vercel provides). The service worker from US-010 can double as the PWA service worker. Cache strategy: network-first for API calls, cache-first for static assets.

userStories.title: Demo Mode + Polish + Documentation
userStories.description: As a new visitor, I want to see the app working immediately, and the app should be polished for production.
acceptanceCriteria: Demo sites shown on dashboard for unauthenticated users with real weather data from Open-Meteo
Landing section on dashboard for unauthenticated users: app description, feature highlights, Sign in to add your own sites CTA

Updated README with: project overview, Turso setup (turso db create, turso db tokens create), NextAuth config (OAuth app setup), VAPID key generation (npx web-push generate-vapid-keys), Vercel deployment guide with env vars, local development instructions
.env.example complete with all required variables and comments
All pages have proper meta tags and Open Graph data
Consistent favicon and PWA icons
Error boundaries on all routes
Loading skeletons on all data-fetching pages
404 page styled consistently
Remove all references to old JSON file storage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Demo mode should NOT require a database - use hardcoded site coordinates and fetch weather client-side or server-side without caching. This way the public demo works even before setting up Turso.
